Patch Set 5:

I think we still need https://review.whamcloud.com/26868 for existing ZFS 0.6.x systems, and as an emergency fallback in case the "netfree" ends up being "netloss" due to ChangeLog usage, and dnode freeing doesn't actually free blocks due to shared blocks in the metadnode.

Patch Set 5:

not that I'm totally against that patch, but I stil not sure I understand the difference.. 
this is what I read in the sources:

 * Normally, we don't allow the last 3.2% (1/(2^spa_slop_shift)) of space in
 * the pool to be consumed.  This ensures that we don't run the pool
 * completely out of space, due to unaccounted changes (e.g. to the MOS).
 * It also limits the worst-case time to allocate space.  If we have
 * less than this amount of free space, most ZPL operations (e.g. write,
 * create) will return ENOSPC.

netfree allows to use a half of that 3.2%, which is a lot, AFAIU.

please, clarify.

It need not be in this patch, but other operations critical to MDT startup might need examination.  An example is writing the config logs received from the MGS.  If MDT runs out of space and is shut down, the admin may not be able to start it again.  This occurred on our test system.

These startup transactions could be marked netfree, in my opinion, as long as the amount of space they could occupy is small and fixed.  Some ZFS administrative commands (e.g. destroying a dataset or a snapshot) allow use of the "slop" space for similar reasons.

commit.subject: LU-8856 osd: mark specific transactions netfree
commit.message:
LU-8856 osd: mark specific transactions netfree

osd-zfs should mark some transactions netfree. this means those transactions
are expected to release space (rather than consume) and for this kind of
transaction half of reserved space is available.
